mappings.rationaleAI: Edison's development of the phonograph, light bulb, and electrical power distribution system, plus his Menlo Park laboratory as the first industrial research facility, addresses the history of technological sciences (A) and electrical engineering (B.2).
Tesla's development of AC systems, the polyphase motor, and wireless power transmission are central to the history of electrical and electronics engineering (A, B.2).
Semiconductor physics and the invention of the transistor enabled the electronics revolution, central to electrical and electronics engineering (B.2).
The history of automata from Hero of Alexandria to Jacquet-Droz covers the history of technological sciences (A) and bionics through the mimicry of nature in mechanical devices (D.1).
The Industrial Revolution's key innovations in textile manufacturing, steam power, iron and steel production, and the factory system are foundational to the history of the technological sciences (A).
Artificial intelligence covers neural networks and computational models of cognition that connect to bionics and the use of the human brain as a paradigm for computer memory devices (D.1).
The Antikythera Mechanism demonstrates sophisticated ancient mechanical engineering, relevant to the history of the technological sciences (A).
Superconductivity's applications in MRI machines, particle accelerators, and maglev trains connect to electrical and electronics engineering (B.2).
Complexity theory's treatment of emergent behavior, network theory, and self-organization connects to systems engineering and operations research (D.2).
